weekly_summary: This week focuses on re-establishing a regular running rhythm after 8 weeks of severely inconsistent training. With NO HR data available, we're using pace-based zones and perceived exertion. Target 4 runs over 6 days, totaling ~22 km, spread across easy aerobic efforts. The goal is to rebuild habit and aerobic base before adding intensity. All runs should feel conversational (easy breathing) — no tempo or speed work this week.
coaching_notes: You've had a difficult 8 weeks with very low training volume (58% below Week 1 levels). This week is about CONSISTENCY, not intensity. We're targeting 22 km across 4 runs — much easier than your 7-hour budget, but realistic given recent pattern. Once you prove you can hit 4 runs/week reliably, we'll add distance and intensity. All runs at conversational effort (pace ~6:00–6:20 min/km based on recent data). No lactate threshold or VO2 max work until HR data is available. Enable HR monitoring immediately — this is essential for safe progression to marathon training.
recovery_flags: Large training gaps in weeks 2–5 suggest potential injury, illness, or life stress — monitor for recurring issues
Only 2 runs in 14 days (Jun 22–25) is unsustainable for marathon prep — address commitment/barriers
No strength or mobility work evident in Strava data — add 1 session/week to reduce injury risk at higher volumes
next_week_preview: Week 2 (Jun 29 – Jul 5): If consistency achieved this week (4+ runs), progress to 5 runs/week with one slightly longer run (10–11 km). Target 28–30 km total. Introduce one easy-paced 'long run' session on Saturday. Continue easy aerobic focus until HR monitoring is active.

days.title: Recovery & Setup Day
days.description: Complete rest day or 10–15 min light walk/mobility. Use this to plan the week, set up HR monitor (if available), and prepare mentally for returning to consistent training.
days.notes: PRIORITY: Acquire HR monitoring device (Garmin watch, Polar chest strap, or Apple Watch) for all future runs.

days.title: Easy Aerobic Base
days.description: 5.5 km easy run at conversational pace (~6:20 min/km). Focus on relaxed form, steady breathing, and enjoyment. This is the foundation — not about speed.
days.notes: If HR monitor is active: target 65–75% MaxHR. Without it: aim for 'easy conversation' breathing.

days.title: Active Recovery
days.description: Rest day or optional 15–20 min easy walk. Focus on sleep, hydration, and nutrition.
days.notes: Use this time to reflect: Are there barriers preventing consistent training? Address them now.

days.title: Easy Aerobic Base
days.description: 6.0 km easy run at ~6:20 min/km. Slightly longer than Tuesday but same effort level. Maintain conversational pace.
days.notes: This matches your recent running pace. If HR monitor active: stay in Zone 2.

days.title: Recovery Day
days.description: Complete rest or 10 min light stretching. Prepare mentally and physically for the weekend.
days.notes: Ensure adequate sleep (7–9 hours). This is when fitness is built.

days.title: Foundation Long Run
days.description: 5.0 km easy run at ~6:20 min/km. This is your 'long run' for the week — not long in absolute terms, but establishing the pattern. Finish feeling fresh.
days.notes: Next week we'll extend this to 10–11 km. For now, focus on completing the distance comfortably.

days.title: Complete Rest or Light Mobility
days.description: Rest day. Optional: 20–30 min light yoga, foam rolling, or strength work (bodyweight: squats, lunges, core). This prepares you for higher weekly volumes.
days.notes: Week summary: 22 km across 4 runs. Total ~2.3 hours. You're still below 7-hour budget, but the goal is consistency first, then we scale volume.
